definitions: The substance of the land surface; soil.
The planet on which we live; the world.
example: Made of rammed earth - the soil excavated from the site - the wall alleviated the need to buy soil or use wood to fence the property.

definitions: The quality of being worthless or futile.
Excessive pride in or admiration of one's own appearance or achievements.
example: the vanity of human wishes

definitions: Say again something one has already said.
Do (something) again or more than once.
example: I may be repeating what Armin wrote, but I'll say it anyway.

definitions: A set of proposals or terms offered or agreed as a whole.
An object or group of objects wrapped in paper or packed in a box.
example: It had wonderful pictures and images from the timeframe, a good essay by Mark Lewis who is a leading Beatles expert, and it's a very good package.

definitions: (of troops) suddenly attack and capture (a building or other place) by means of force.
Move angrily or forcefully in a specified direction.
example: This was a facile victory for the girls who stormed into the game from the tip-off and dominated their opponents in every sector to seal a great win.

definitions: Make (a bill or other proposal) law.
Put into practice (an idea or suggestion)
example: The proposed law - expected to be enacted in July - also would compel companies to prevent workplace bad behavior.

definitions: Endure (an ordeal or difficulty)
(of a person) carry (someone or something)
example: He said it was never the triumph, or the sensation of winning that drove me, just he couldn't bear himself when he lost.

definitions: Flooring composed of wooden blocks arranged in a geometric pattern.
The ground floor of a theatre or auditorium, especially the orchestra pit.
example: He also retained the herringbone parquet flooring and double-height sash windows, blending the existing elements of the building with sumptuous fabrics and walnut panelling.

definitions: Indicate the position of.
Write a word or symbol on (an object) in order to give information.
example: If the Erskine scheme comes to pass, it will mark a significant change in fortunes for similar proposals.

definitions: A large primate that lacks a tail, including the gorilla, chimpanzees, orangutan, and gibbons.
An unintelligent or clumsy person.
example: Some apes, such as gorillas, showed no self-recognition whatsoever.

definitions: A red-hot iron or poker.
A metal plate heated and placed over food to brown it.
example: Place gratineed stacks under a salamander or broiler until top is browned.

definitions: An official record of events during the voyage of a ship or aircraft.

An apparatus for determining the speed of a ship, originally one consisting of a float attached to a knotted line that is wound on a reel, the distance run out in a certain time being used as an estimate of the vessel's speed.
example: Traditionally, a vessel's speed was determined using a log and line - a float on the end of a line knotted at precise intervals and tossed overboard.

definitions: Commit (a person or organization) by a solemn promise.
Give as security on a loan.
example: He's pledged to execute every family that will not bend a knee to him.

definitions: (in the UK) a member of the former Women's Royal Naval Service.
Any of a number of small songbirds that resemble the true wrens in size or appearance.
example: Other jobs followed: wages clerk, waitress, shop assistant and then, when she was 17 and desperate to escape from home, she joined the Wrens.

definitions: An arrangement or product (such as a future, option, or warrant) whose value derives from and is dependent on the value of an underlying asset, such as a commodity, currency, or security.
Something which is based on another source.
example: the derivatives market

definitions: A point at which the amplitude of vibration in a standing wave system is zero.
A point in a network or diagram at which lines or pathways intersect or branch.
example: The first switch group is formed by switches, which are connected to nodes between the resistors.

definitions: An autonomous program on the internet or another network that can interact with systems or users.
(chiefly in science fiction) a robot.
example: A further step imbeds images into the address, which thwarts the most persistent bots.

definitions: A set of tickets, stamps, matches, samples of cloth, etc., bound together.
A bound set of blank sheets for writing in.
example: Once, when he was driving with his fiancée, a traffic policeman stopped him and took out a ticket book.

definitions: Begin or be reckoned from a particular point in time or space.
(of event or process) happen or come into being.
example: My brother kept the vehicle going, and the storm eased as suddenly as it had started.

definitions: A site for depositing rubbish.
An unpleasant or dreary place.
example: why are you living in a dump like this?

definitions: Describing an action or situation that was done repeatedly or existed for a period in the past.
Be or become familiar with (someone or something) through experience.
example: Because most houses used to own one or two cows that they would keep in a byre near the house.

definitions: Either of two bushy North American plants with silvery-grey leaves.
An aromatic plant whose greyish-green leaves are used as a culinary herb, native to southern Europe and the Mediterranean.
example: Cleveland and white sage, creosote bush and brittlebush, pine and redwood, for instance, are filled with resins and release their scents on warm afternoons.

definitions: A place where roads or railway lines cross.
A place at which one may safely cross something, especially a street.
example: Posters will be put up at all of the railway stations, railway crossings, access points and known trespass locations.

definitions: Agreement or harmony between people or groups.
A chord that is pleasing or satisfactory in itself.
example: The programme also assumes that there is something prior to music, some experience ruling it: music's discords are as if distress, concords as if relief.

definitions: The movement of a matador's cape away from a charging bull.
A cloth supposedly impressed with an image of Christ's face.
example: The veronica represents Christ's face and, by synecdoche, his entire body.

definitions: (in tennis and other racket sports) hit the ball or shuttlecock to begin play for each point of a game.
Be of use in achieving or satisfying.
example: He got the message, gave Gracie and me a salute, and ran back to his game, serving the ball and acing it.

definitions: The quality or state of being unassuming in the estimation of one's abilities.
The quality of being relatively moderate, limited, or small in amount, rate, or level.
example: This may be false modesty or perhaps just a simple human failing.

definitions: A flat, typically rectangular, vertical surface; a panel.
A set of facts or figures systematically displayed, especially in columns.
example: The first column of the table indicates the signs of the zodiac.

definitions: Publish or broadcast (a story or play) in regular instalments.
Arrange (something) in a series.
example: Something more along the lines of an instant messaging chat room session, where the alternating blog entries are serialized, would be better.

definitions: A steady, continuous stream or supply of something.
The action or fact of moving along in a steady, continuous stream.
example: The very next day, a freak summer windstorm clogged the water flow with leaves, flooding the flanking fields in London's Hyde Park.

definitions: The capacity of an object or substance to withstand great force or pressure.
The quality or state of being physically strong.
example: In Japan, he sees political parties solely occupied in securing power and preoccupied in increasing strength and influence.

definitions: Put right; correct.
Convert (alternating current) to direct current.
example: Physicists have built a novel device that relies on electron spin to rectify current.

definitions: Suppose to be the case, without proof.
Begin to have (a specified quality, appearance, or extent)
example: Far be it from him to assume that marriage was supposed to be fun.

definitions: A place where buses, trains, or other vehicles are housed and maintained and from which they are dispatched for service.
A place where recruits are trained or other troops are assembled.
example: There was also a bomb attack on a house and a taxi depot was burned out in the city.

definitions: One of three children or animals born at the same birth.
A set or succession of three similar things.
example: The verses of the Irish chapbooks are all in couplets but, in using the liberty line, a pair of couplets is transformed into a triplet plus an isolated line.

definitions: A small glass or measure of alcohol.
A horse of a small breed, especially one below 15 hands (or 14 hands 2 inches).
example: The riding school provides group and individual classes for equestrians of all ages and abilities who get to saddle up one of 11 horses and ponies.

definitions: Be aware of (a person or object) through touching or being touched.
Have a belief or impression, especially without an identifiable reason.
example: Was his life at the ranch so difficult he felt it necessary to leave?

definitions: (in poker) a continuous sequence of five cards.
A part of something that is not curved or bent, especially a straight section of a racecourse.
example: We jogged the corners of the track and sprinted the straights.

definitions: An act of heaving.
A sideways displacement in a fault.
example: Zane was struggling, his breaths coming in short heaves and his face turning red.

definitions: The space (usually measured in degrees) between two intersecting lines or surfaces at or close to the point where they meet.
Each of the four cardinal points of a chart, from which the first, fourth, seventh, and tenth houses extend anticlockwise respectively.
example: The final grand trine comes from the dark angle or the 4th house - the lowest point in heaven.

definitions: A large longhorn beetle whose larvae bore tunnels in the wood of injured or recently felled trees, producing an audible chewing sound.
A person who saws timber for a living.
example: In 1754 an immigrant sawyer, who had begun a business in New York City, advertised that he had a good house for keeping timber out of the weather.

definitions: An extensive unbroken surface area of something.
A broad flat piece of material such as metal or glass.
example: Loch Affric is a lovely sheet of water among trees

definitions: The provision of fresh air to a room, building, etc.
The supply of air to the lungs, especially by means of a machine or device used to support or replace the breathing of a person who is ill, injured, or anaesthetized.
example: Looking ahead of him, he saw a ventilation grate fall from the ceiling.

definitions: A class or order regarded as forming part of the body politic, in particular (in Britain), one of the three groups constituting Parliament, now the Lords spiritual (the heads of the Church), the Lords temporal (the peerage), and the Commons. They are also known as the three estates.
A particular state, period, or condition in life.
example: the spiritual welfare of all estates of men

definitions: Speak to (a person or an assembly)
Think about and begin to deal with (an issue or problem)
example: Meanwhile, Joss was scolded by her mom for addressing President Bush as George.

definitions: Fail (an examination)
(especially of a vehicle) move in a fast and uncontrolled manner.
example: Not many people plough Greats at 21 and become a professor of Latin at 33.

definitions: A regular short, sharp sound, especially that made by a clock or watch.
The smallest recognized amount by which a price of a security or future may fluctuate.
example: The second will be defined to be so many ticks of the standard clock.

definitions: A means of catching someone; a trap.
A piece of open-meshed material made of twine, cord, or something similar, used typically for catching fish or other animals.
example: Once those animals rot the fishing net might float back up to the surface again and start ghost fishing once again.

definitions: The process of displaying content from the screen of a computer, phone, or other device on another screen, usually via a wireless connection.
The reflection or replication of something.
example: the mirroring of their words seems designed to try and heal the divide

definitions: A thin, flat piece of wood or other stiff material used for various purposes.
A long, thin, flat piece of wood or other hard material, used for floors or other building purposes.
example: Rusted sheet metal, asphaltic roofing, old boards, or slate tiles are other roofing alternatives.

definitions: An avocado.
A monetary unit of Macao, equal to one hundredth of a pataca.
example: The official monetary currency of Macau is the Pataca (or MOP$) which is divided into 100 avos.

definitions: A layer of food on which other foods are served.
A stratum or layer of rock.
example: The baked lamb is served on the bed of rice liberally garnished with almonds and ghee.

definitions: Divide (an area) into triangles for surveying purposes.
Form into a triangle or triangles.
example: I and others showed that any polygon could be triangulated with non-obtuse triangles.

definitions: A rank of officer in the army and the US air force, above captain and below lieutenant colonel.
A major organization or competition.
example: The officer in front, a major, bent and jabbed him with his swagger stick.

definitions: An unmodulated reference signal transmitted with another signal for the purposes of control or synchronization.
A television or radio programme made to test audience reaction with a view to the production of a series.
example: While the tracking sensitivity has been greatly enhanced by having a pilot signal, the extremely long code chosen has made it impractical to use it for acquisition.

definitions: Put together (two or more numbers or amounts) to calculate their total value.
Join (something) to something else so as to increase the size, number, or amount.
example: I've started a petition so if you would like to add your name, email me

definitions: A set of ropes forming part of the standing rigging of a sailing boat and supporting the mast or topmast.
A length of cloth or an enveloping garment in which a dead person is wrapped for burial.
example: We had been instructed to steer by manipulating the shroud lines of the parachute, and I tried that without success.

definitions: Empty space.
Lack of intelligence or understanding.
example: It is a deft balancing act, measuring blank vacancy with an equally manic intensity.

definitions: Provide (soldiers) with accommodation in a building or set of buildings.
Give support and encouragement to.
example: Forces were barracked there until well after the end of WWII.

definitions: A psychic bid.
Psychedelic rock music.
example: Whilst psyches are a legal and legitimate part of the game of bridge, and as such, are permissible within BCL, the guidelines of the WBF state that "the laws of Duplicate Contract Bridge 1997 permit a player to make a psychic call provided that such calls are not based on a partnership understanding."

definitions: The material or protoplasm within a living cell, excluding the nucleus.
The colourless fluid part of blood, lymph, or milk, in which corpuscles or fat globules are suspended.
example: Aquaporins are water channel proteins that are expressed in various membrane compartments of plant cells, including the plasma and vacuolar membranes.

definitions: A sudden or brief manifestation or occurrence of something.
Ostentatious stylishness or display of wealth.
example: Despite all the flash in Las Vegas, this is the one place we experience true wonder.

definitions: A disc jockey.
An enthusiast or participant in a specified activity.
example: Sports talk-radio jocks hammered at the stars for betraying the public trust.

definitions: Join (something) to something else so as to increase the size, number, or amount.
Put together (two or more numbers or amounts) to calculate their total value.
example: This was computed by adding the numbers against each of the 25 stories.

definitions: Look at all parts of (something) carefully in order to detect some feature.
Analyse the metre of (a line of verse) by reading with the emphasis on its rhythm or by examining the pattern of feet or syllables.
example: She straightened out the folded papers and scanned the letter.

definitions: A person who believes that eschatological prophecies are still to be fulfilled.
A person who studies the future and makes predictions about it based on current trends.
example: And to get a slant on how to bring the big picture into focus, we turned to business futurists who have a knack for forecasting economic and technological trends.

definitions: A person's head.
A thick, flat, savoury cake with a soft, porous texture, made from a yeast mixture cooked on a griddle and eaten toasted and buttered.
example: We shared two hot toasted crumpets and a cherry scone.

definitions: A sweet spread or conserve made from fruit and sugar boiled to a thick consistency.
An instance of a thing seizing or becoming stuck.
example: A selection of delicate pastries, banana slices, glazed fudge, lemon tarts, scones with clotted cream and jam, and boiled Welsh fruit cake were also on offer.

definitions: A final point in space or time; an end or extremity.
The end of a railway or other transport route, or a station at such a point; a terminal.
example: When we got to this terminus station, our train remained on the right-side track.

definitions: Behave towards or deal with in a certain way.
Negotiate terms with someone, especially an opponent.
example: He has 60 days from the receipt of the recommendations and three options in treating with the authority: accept, reject or modify.

definitions: The regular custom attracted by a shop, restaurant, etc.
The support given by a patron.
example: My own patronage of his shop has been steadfast over these 27 years since that initial purchase.

definitions: The theory that the design of an object should be determined by its function rather than by aesthetic considerations, and that anything practically designed will be inherently beautiful.
The theory that all aspects of a society serve a function and are necessary for the survival of that society.
example: In what is easily, in my opinion, one of the best essays on contemporary design in recent years, Boym persuasively argues that the days of good design based purely on functionalism are squarely in the past.

definitions: Small width in relation to length.
Limited extent, amount, or scope.
example: Because of the height and narrowness of most hospital beds, the consequences of a newborn falling from an adult bed can be serious.

definitions: Any of various forms of team game involving kicking (and in some cases also handling) a ball, in particular (in the UK) soccer or (in the US) American football.
A ball used in football, either round (as in soccer) or oval (as in rugby and American football) and typically made of leather or plastic and filled with compressed air.
example: When I was 16 years old, my Mississippi high school football team had a game in New Orleans.

definitions: Sustained effort; hard work.
The action of applying something to a surface.
example: So if it works, the figures are just, and can be worked into an understanding with a great deal of patient application.

definitions: Each of the four chief fluids of the body (blood, phlegm, yellow bile (choler), and black bile (melancholy)) that were thought to determine a person's physical and mental qualities by the relative proportions in which they were present.
A mood or state of mind.
example: Traditionally, disease is seen as the effect of bad winds and an imbalance of the four humors of the body.

definitions: The principal sail of a ship, especially the lowest sail on the mainmast in a square-rigged vessel.
A principal pipe carrying water or gas to buildings, or taking sewage from them.
example: I told him not to touch the main, and to let go the port jib sheet and haul it in starboard side.

definitions: A tough elastic polymeric substance made from the latex of a tropical plant or synthetically.
A piece of rubber used for erasing pencil or ink marks.
example: To avoid rounding over the corners use a hard rubber or wooden sanding block, not a power sander.

definitions: Guidance or recommendations offered with regard to prudent future action.
A formal notice of a financial transaction.
example: An offsider is hauling together the thousands of different legal advices and papers published on the web.

definitions: The process of decaying.
A process of deterioration; a decline in standards.
example: While most of the world is obsessed with sell-by dates, freshness and ripeness, the sleepy hamlet of Sauternes sits 30 miles south of Bordeaux, obsessed with rot, decay and fungus.

definitions: A simultaneous attack on two or more pieces by one.
A flash of forked lightning.
example: A fork occurs when one piece attacks two or more enemy units at the same time.

definitions: A thing which opposes or prevents something else.
The curved part of the stern of a ship projecting aft above the waterline.
example: Jans decided to begin staging the travel shows as a counter to dull presentations he'd witnessed.

definitions: Beat (cream, eggs, or other food) into a froth.
Beat (a person or animal) with a whip or similar instrument, especially as a punishment or to urge them on.
example: The man grabs the boy, and whips him to within an inch of his life.

definitions: An established itinerary of events or venues used for a particular activity, typically involving sport or public performance.
A roughly circular line, route, or movement that starts and finishes at the same place.
example: This will include professional tennis coaching, co-ordination circuits and fun team sports and competitions.

definitions: A fabric or yarn with a sheen or gloss.
A thin coating containing unoxidized metal which gives an iridescent glaze to ceramics.
example: lustre jugs

definitions: A baby.
A sexually attractive young woman.
example: But the main attractions at the club were the waitresses, all hot babes dressed only in lingerie made out of lettuce.

definitions: A curse.
An official or legal prohibition.
example: Despite the official ban on direct trade with China, cross-strait trade soared into record territory, economics officials said yesterday.

definitions: A reservoir for oil in an oil lamp.
A receptacle in a church for the water used in baptism, typically a free-standing stone structure.
example: Most fonts were made of stone, although other materials such as marble, lead, copper, and bronze were also used.

definitions: The part of an electric circuit that is at a lower electrical potential than another part designated as having zero electrical potential.
A number less than zero.
example: Welding is done with direct current, electrode negative (straight polarity).

definitions: A sudden loud, sharp noise.
An act or instance of having sex.
example: Young Andrew Wilson, until a bang on the head necessitated his withdrawal, again played very well.

definitions: The southern part of the world or of a specified country, region, or town.
The direction towards the point of the horizon 90° clockwise from east, or the point on the horizon itself.
example: It turned and began to head in a direct line to the south.

definitions: Provide (access or an opportunity)
Present or proffer (something) for (someone) to accept or reject as desired.
example: Furthermore, the patients were unaware that prayers were being offered on their behalf.

definitions: Hard dull work.
A crushing or grating sound or motion.
example: His decision to give up the daily grind was made when he took 10 weeks off in the summer of 2001 to ponder the future.

definitions: A plant that grows in a specified way.
A song or piece of music that is initially unimpressive but becomes more appealing when heard several times.
example: It's not a grower—the songs are either too fiddly or too slight.

definitions: A steam locomotive of 2-6-0 wheel arrangement.
A member of the Muslim dynasty of Mongol origin founded by the successors of Tamerlane, which ruled much of India from the 16th to the 19th century.
example: The Mughals of India were mighty rulers, for three centuries, especially in their golden days for six generations, from

definitions: The joint between the thigh and the lower leg in humans.
An angled piece of wood or metal frame used to connect and support the beams and timbers of a wooden ship.
example: The deck and hull are through bolted on an inward flange and structural knees and bulkheads are securely attached.

definitions: A person who smokes tobacco regularly.
A person or device that smokes fish or meat.
example: when the fish is ready, put it in the warm smoker

definitions: A 17th-century Scottish Presbyterian.
A supporter of the American side during the War of American Independence.
example: What was crucial for the post-revolutionary Whigs that comprised the Scottish Enlightenment was not Locke but rather their own institutional status.

definitions: An extra benefit supplementing an employee's money wage or salary, for example a company car, private healthcare, etc.
The border or outer edges of an area or group.
example: we offer the highest salary and fringes in the country

definitions: A broad inlet of the sea where the land curves inwards.
An evergreen Mediterranean shrub with deep green leaves and purple berries. Its aromatic leaves are used in cooking and were formerly used to make triumphal crowns for victors.
example: Anyone that has a sunny patch of ground or a window-box can grow these herbs of parsley, garlic, basil, bay laurel and oregano.

definitions: The series of letters and figures identifying a motor vehicle, assigned on registration and displayed on a number plate.
The action or process of registering or of being registered.
example: Most queries fielded by One Family related to legal issues relating to guardianship, divorce, separation, birth registration, and social welfare issues.

definitions: (in soccer, hockey, basketball, etc.) kick, hit, or throw the ball or puck in an attempt to score a goal.
Kill or wound (a person or animal) with a bullet or arrow.
example: Our game plan in the Finals was to take the puck wide, shoot, and go for rebounds.

definitions: A pop-up shop or other business.
A pop-up menu or other utility.
example: Years ago, you said, pop-ups were considered a place to test a new retail concept.

definitions: The solid surface of the earth.
Solid particles, especially of coffee, which form a residue; sediment.
example: It was indeed watery, and I could see some dark coffee grounds floating at the bottom of my cup.

definitions: The action of providing or supplying something for use.
An amount or thing supplied or provided.
example: I know we have had experiences of Muslim officers at training school not being able to eat during the day, because they did not have the correct food provisions; that has been addressed.

definitions: An official record of a student's work, showing courses taken and grades achieved.
A written or printed version of material originally presented in another medium.
example: During this time, I received official transcripts of all my course work at the University of Massachusetts.

definitions: The shooting of projectiles from weapons, especially bullets from guns.
A burning sensation.
example: However, in the hail of bullets and recoilless rifle fire, over fifty hostages had been killed.

definitions: A place where two planes of rock meet at an angle of between 60° and 120°.
An angle formed by two plane faces.
example: We meant to simply repeat his route, but several pitches up, where that route goes up and right into blocky cracks, we angled up and left into the obvious dihedral that shoots to the top of the wall.

definitions: Pass into a specified state, especially one of separation or disunion.
Move or travel towards or into a place thought of as near or familiar to the speaker.
example: The infamous discipline seems to be coming apart at the seams.

definitions: Shape or reshape (a hat) on a mould.
Impress text or a design on (a book cover).
example: He is well cast and carries the part like a well blocked hat, (though his own hat sometimes got the better of him).

definitions: A rounded eye-like marking on an animal, especially on the wing of a butterfly or moth.
A light-sensitive pigmented spot on the bodies of invertebrates such as flatworms, starfishes, and microscopic crustaceans, and also in some unicellular organisms.
example: The beautiful eyespots on butterfly wings are thought to rely on the principles described above, although involving more morphogens.

definitions: A battle line behind the front line to support it and make good its losses.
A medical treatment or therapy used in support of another, or as a more drastic measure if the primary treatment is ineffective.
example: No ideal second-line treatment has been established if the initial therapy fails to eradicate the organism.

definitions: Practise (a trade or profession).
Act according to (an instruction or precept)
example: We know from grim experience that footballers slavishly follow the lead of their manager.

definitions: Bring (people or groups with particular characteristics or needs) into equal participation in or membership of a social group or institution.
Combine (one thing) with another to form a whole.

definitions: A large sieve or riddle, especially one for sorting substances such as grain or coal into different sizes.
A fixed or movable upright partition used to divide a room, give shelter from draughts, heat, or light, or to provide concealment or privacy.
example: The freshman point guard went straight behind a second screen to find some room deep in the right corner.

definitions: A set of similar units of equipment, typically when connected together.
A container consisting of one or more cells, in which chemical energy is converted into electricity and used as a source of power.
example: You can see why there is so much excitement around fuel cells right now - fuel cells solve the battery problem.

definitions: The product or offspring of a person or place.
The mycelium of a fungus, especially a cultivated mushroom.
example: She was like the wild, caffeinated, blonde spawn of Satan.

definitions: A wooden frame over which a canvas is spread and tautened ready for painting.
A framework of two poles with a long piece of canvas slung between them, used for carrying sick, injured, or dead people.
example: It's a miracle how they carry those stretchers but they get through even though it takes ten men all day to move one stretcher case back three or four miles.

definitions: (of people) gather together in one place for a common purpose.
Fit together the separate component parts of (a machine or other object)
example: Since the mid-1980s, Costa Rica has become a center for factories that assemble garments, electronic components, and other goods for export.

definitions: Allow (something) to move, act, or flow freely.
Allow or enable to escape from confinement; set free.
example: He released the remaining political prisoners and restored democratic rule.

definitions: A strip or encircling area that is different in nature or composition from its surroundings.
A strip of leather or other material worn, typically round the waist, to support or hold in clothes or to carry weapons.
example: A similar observation led Hart to average his data for species composition in circumpolar belts.

definitions: An intensive or sudden military attack.
A form of chess in which moves must be made at very short intervals.
example: Back in the mid-Nineties, I played in a blitz tournament in Germany.

definitions: An act or instance of blow-drying hair.
An occasion when a tyre on a vehicle bursts or an electric fuse melts.

definitions: Detract from.
Deviate from (a set of rules or agreed form of behaviour)
example: To view s 104 in this way is not to deny the mandatory nature of the duty, nor to derogate from previous authorities - and there is reference to them.

definitions: A vertical line or plane.
An upright structure.
example: When doing this turn, the swimmer would roll to his side, keeping his shoulders just shy of the vertical.

definitions: A room or hall for lectures with seats in tiers.
A building or outdoor area in which plays and other dramatic performances are given.
example: When was the last time a boy held a door open for me coming out of a lecture theatre?

definitions: Pulsation or throbbing, especially of the heart.
A defeat in a competitive situation.
example: It was indeed a battle for the fittest and reputations did take a severe beating once the competitions gained momentum.

definitions: Break free from confinement or control.
Fail to be noticed or remembered by (someone)
example: This was lead by a female teacher whose name escapes me.

definitions: (of a person) move suddenly and quickly in a specified way.
Start (a vehicle) using jump leads.
example: I jumped his Buick from the Bronco's battery.

definitions: A primary division of a pinnate leaf, especially of a fern.
Any of a number of animal structures resembling fins or wings.
example: Lepidoteris fronds typically have so-called intercalary pinnules along the rachis between the primary pinnae, and are covered with the characteristic blisters mentioned above.

definitions: A suspicious or unfriendly look.
A very wide-angle lens with a field of vision covering up to 180°, the scale being reduced towards the edges.
example: There was everything from a professional 80-200 mm zoom to a 15 mm fisheye with built-in color filter wheel.

definitions: A small chapel, especially for private worship.
The art or practice of formal speaking in public.
example: My room key came with an invitation to use the oratory in Kohne Hall, open 24 hours a day.

definitions: A downward bend of a hat brim.
A lazy, drooping posture or movement.
example: My back ached and for the first time, I recognized the slouch in my posture.

definitions: A person who keeps watch, especially a soldier or other person assigned to protect a person or to control access to a place.
An official who rides on and is in general charge of a train.
example: Andrew waited until none of the guards were watching and quickly dove into the the Humvee.

definitions: A carnivorous mammal of the dog family with a pointed muzzle and bushy tail, proverbial for its cunning.
A cunning or sly person.
example: The virus is carried by a number of wild animals, including coyotes, foxes, and some wolves.

definitions: The back of the thigh or the thighs and buttocks.
Salted or smoked meat from the upper part of a pig's leg.
example: This low rep range is essential to developing the hams and glutes at the expense of unwanted bodyfat.

definitions: The action or state of flexing.
A boastful statement or display.
example: Glass is then placed over the solar cells to eliminate flex or physical damage to the solar cells.

definitions: The process of or management involved in making a film, play, or record.
The action of making or manufacturing from components or raw materials, or the process of being so manufactured.
example: There is likely to be a leveling off in total U.S. production in years to come.

definitions: The chief magistrate of a French or other European town.
(in Scotland) the civic head of some regional Scottish councils, analogous to a mayor in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland.
example: A defection from Labour ranks to the Scottish Socialist Party in Renfrewshire a month ago means that Labour can only win votes with the casting vote of the provost.

definitions: A constant factor or ratio.
A number used as a divisor for considering numbers in sets, numbers being considered congruent when giving the same remainder when divided by a particular modulus.
example: His first notebook was on hypergeometric series, continued fractions, singular moduli, and many branches of number theory.

definitions: A widespread North American shrike, having mainly grey plumage with a black eyestripe, wings, and tail.
A reddish-brown turtle with a very large head, occurring chiefly in warm seas.
example: Both the green turtle, which can weigh up to 205 kilograms, and the loggerhead turtle are now classified as endangered by the International Union for the Conservation of Nature.

definitions: The action, especially of a man, of showing one's genitals briefly in public.
A strip of metal used to stop water penetrating the junction of a roof with another surface.
example: Site-built or factory-supplied flashings are used to seal the roofing around the curb.

definitions: A key fact, point, or idea to be remembered, typically one emerging from a discussion or meeting.
(in football and hockey) an act of regaining the ball or puck from the opposing team.
example: The real takeaway you get from the film is the same one his daughters touchingly acknowledge in the denouement - there's never anything wrong with making a stand against social injustice.

definitions: Turn a public office or a position of trust to private advantage.
Cheat; betray.
example: At this point, with all the hurt and pain of being jilted and jobbed by the BCS system, that's all the Miami Hurricanes can hold on to.

definitions: Bring into disrepute or danger by indiscreet, foolish, or reckless behaviour.
Settle a dispute by mutual concession.
example: Very intense training may temporarily compromise your immune system, also making you more susceptible.

definitions: Remove the flesh adhering to (a skin or hide)
Stimulate (a hound or hawk) to hunt by feeding it a piece of flesh from a recently killed animal.
example: chemicals remove the hair and preserving salts, and then the hides are fleshed

definitions: The protective covering of an animal or plant.
The rotating coil or coils of a dynamo or electric motor.
example: Sixth, distinct increase of power by employment of the device of a so-called armature made of the same metal with which the physicists are accustomed to surround resinous and vitreous bodies.

definitions: A country or state.
The part of the earth's surface that is not covered by water.
example: The African elephant is the largest living land animal and weighs up to 5,400 kg.

definitions: An idea or plan put forward for consideration.
The action of calling up an idea in someone's mind by associating it with other things.
example: These are simply positive suggestions the writer gives to herself while in hypnosis.

definitions: Make less tedious or monotonous by the introduction of variety.
Make (something) stand out.
example: Yet, the beams prove futile in relieving the oppressing darkness.

definitions: A promontory.
Thin, soft, plain muslin, used in bookbinding for joining the spine of a book to its cover.
example: The point of the needle darts back and forth, teasing the weave of the fine muslin, mull or cotton.

definitions: Cover something with liberal amounts of (a substance)
Form or cause to form a lather.
example: I pulled out a bottle of sunblock from my bag and unscrewed the cap, beginning to lather it on.

definitions: A protective paper or cardboard cover for a record, CD, or DVD.
The part of a garment that wholly or partly covers a person's arm.
example: The outer cable conductor is enclosed by a contact sleeve which has a plurality of radially resilient segments.

definitions: The quality of being easy to cut or chew; succulence.
Gentleness and kindness; kindliness.
example: Music, like a small act of tenderness, aids romance and is certainly used in one way or another by most of us.

definitions: A meeting of an official body, especially a legislature, council, or court of law, to conduct its business.
The governing body of a Presbyterian Church.
example: President Danny Terry conducted this session of the business meeting.

definitions: A metal projectile for firing from a rifle, revolver, or other small firearm, typically cylindrical and pointed, and sometimes containing an explosive.
A stick of lipstick (considered separately from the tube in which it is encased)
example: The labs supplied her with “fills”: bullets of lipstick, buttons of lip gloss, and plastic pans of eye shadow.

definitions: A number of events, objects, or people of a similar or related kind coming one after another.
Denoting electrical circuits or components arranged so that the current passes through each successively.
example: the series connection of the two coils

definitions: Any of a number of trees or shrubs with peppermint-scented foliage.
The cultivated Old World plant which yields peppermint leaves or oil.
example: More seasonal and colourful plants such as cherry, Chinese flowering crabapple and pomegranate as well as some fragrant plants such as peppermint, thyme and rosemary dot the park.

definitions: A group of people living in the same place or having a particular characteristic in common.
A group of interdependent plants or animals growing or living together in natural conditions or occupying a specified habitat.
example: A similar picture is seen in the Silurian, with five communities inhabiting the same area and forming concentric belts parallel to what was then the shoreline.

definitions: A quick, short movement up and down.
A bobsleigh.
example: Unlike Garrett's bewildered reaction, Clara only gave a slight bob of her head to acknowledge him.

definitions: A short cut.
Shorts made by cutting off the legs of a pair of jeans or other trousers and leaving the edges unhemmed.
example: She was soaked down, the lines of a bathing suit showing through the old black t-shirt she was wearing with a pair of cargo cut-offs and black Chucks.

definitions: Allocate (a job or duty)
Designate or set (something) aside for a specific purpose.
example: I feel I am an example of midwife services working well with resources being assigned appropriately.

definitions: A gladiolus.
A migratory butterfly with predominantly orange-brown wings and darker markings.
example: While long-tubed painted lady has been long in cultivation, it is still rare.

definitions: A thing comparable to an arm in form or function, typically something that projects from a larger structure.
Each of the two upper limbs of the human body from the shoulder to the hand.
example: Not long after, she felt Elias turn in the sheets and wrap a protecting arm around her.

definitions: Provide with shelter or accommodation.
Provide space for; contain or accommodate.
example: More than 500 students and nurses will be housed in a huge new accommodation block situated in the heart of the city centre.

definitions: A beating or scolding.
The activities in which a particular person engages.
example: Nineteenth-century scholars and 11th-century landowners may not have had much in common, but they did share a profound suspicion of the state and its financial doings.

definitions: An express rifle.
A train that stops at few stations and travels quickly.
example: The maglev train from Shanghai to Pudong airport is already in service, but it looks like the planned Beijing-Shanghai maglev express is a non-starter.

definitions: A thing that is imposed, in particular an unfair or unwelcome demand or burden.
The action or process of imposing something or of being imposed.
example: The central aspect of free trade, as the name implies, is the free flow of goods into and out of countries without any imposition of tariffs.

definitions: A hard or padded protective hat, various types of which are worn by soldiers, police officers, motorcyclists, sports players, and others.
The arched upper part (galea) of the corolla in some flowers, especially those of the mint and orchid families.
example: The wind beat hard at their helmets and clothes.

definitions: Pull or twist out of shape.
Change the form of (an electrical signal or sound wave) during transmission, amplification, or other processing.
example: He heard a voice, twisted and distorted, coursing to his ears.

definitions: Move in or take a twisting or spiral course.
Pass (something) round a thing or person so as to encircle or enfold.
example: The woven threads were wound on a device called a Niddy Noddy or more simply a yarn winder.

definitions: A filled or open sandwich.
(among miners) a friend or workmate.
example: I eventually got up and was given a cup of tea and a bacon buttie.

definitions: A rule defining correct procedure or behaviour in a sport.
A statement of fact, deduced from observation, to the effect that a particular natural or scientific phenomenon always occurs if certain conditions are present.
example: In 1893 Wien stated his displacement law of blackbody radiation spectra at different temperatures.

definitions: The condition of being fenestrate.
The arrangement of windows in a building.
example: Because these new second-story spaces do not touch the original brick enclosing wall, the building's fenestration remains intact.

definitions: The process of making or producing something.
The essential qualities needed for something.
example: But Parker and Stone confirm the actual making of the film has been laborious and exhausting.

definitions: Change (something, typically colour or shade) gradually or step by step.
Arrange in a series or according to a scale.
example: So it's really four graduated hearts, scaled to size, and then of course you'll need some decorative papers like these.

definitions: A lecherous man.
A hardy domesticated ruminant mammal that has backward-curving horns and (in the male) a beard. It is kept for its milk and meat, and noted for its lively behaviour.
example: The crater walls, massive in height and rugged, were the domain of jet-black wild goats who managed to navigate and cling to the rough face.

definitions: Cause (someone or something) to exert the utmost effort.
Hold (something) out towards someone.
example: After mere survival, the primary activity of life is to create more life, to extend itself, to keep going, to procreate.

definitions: Approval, support, or liking for someone or something.
An act of kindness beyond what is due or usual.
example: The government gave us an important quest to fulfill before our execution, but we will not return the favor of kindness!

definitions: A police officer.
The wild cornflower.
example: But this place being open was full of flowers, such lovely azure cornflowers which the people call bluebottles.

definitions: A soft bag with handles for carrying belongings in on a journey.
A handle for holding something by.
example: The arrow should be placed in the centre of the bowstring with the shaft resting on the bow just above your handgrip.

definitions: A zero.
A condition of no signal.
example: A well-designed GPS receiver usually can recover from a deep signal null within approximately one second.

definitions: Fasten or be fastened with a clip or clips.
Cut short or trim (hair, vegetation, etc.) with shears or scissors.
example: He also had light gray hair and was wearing a white over jacket with pens clipped to the right pocket on it.

definitions: Unite or cause to unite.
Make (a garment, blanket, etc.) by interlocking loops of wool or other yarn with knitting needles or on a machine.
example: Small-leaved plants that tolerate close clipping will quickly knit together to form a seamless hedge.

definitions: Apply salve to.
Rescue (a wrecked or disabled ship or its cargo) from loss at sea.
example: More recently, 60 brass shell cases were salved from under a 5.5in gun which had been mounted on the stern.

definitions: An instrument marked with a line parallel to the plane of the horizon for testing whether things are horizontal.
A horizontal plane or line with respect to the distance above or below a given point.
example: In place of an open rear sight on the barrel, a bubble level is fitted to help avoid canting the rifle.

definitions: (in leather-making) the hide of a young or small animal.
A sleep or nap.
example: Then I would make my way back to somewhere I could catch the coach back to Oxford, and try to get a bit of kip on the journey.

definitions: A small, often slightly raised blemish on the skin made dark by a high concentration of melanin.
A small burrowing mammal with dark velvety fur, a long muzzle, and very small eyes, feeding mainly on worms, grubs, and other invertebrates.
example: Even though we're on the edge of a major city, our yard is populated by a variety of mice, moles, squirrels, chipmunks and opossums.

definitions: The forefront or vanguard, especially of technological development.
The foremost edge of an aerofoil, especially a wing or propeller blade.
example: Their lengthened nacelles overlapped the leading edge of the wing and were equipped with a streamlined canopy and cabin in which a single gunner was installed.

definitions: Pawn (something)
Go somewhere for a short time, often without notice.
example: Carter volleyed Carlton into the lead with what looked to be the winner with two minutes remaining but there was still time for Mark Andrews to pop in an equaliser.

definitions: Cocaine.
A member of the Vietcong or the Vietcong collectively.
example: The Viet Cong aka Charlie will be eating in our "mess hall".

definitions: Breathe in repeated short gasps.
Swell or become swollen.
example: It puffed a whiff of smoke and crouched down to their level.

definitions: A bundle of sticks bound together as fuel.
A ball or roll of seasoned chopped liver, baked or fried.
example: His faggots were legendary. We opened in 1994; eight months later, we had a Michelin star, one of the fastest ever to be won.

definitions: The Salvation Army.
The part of a bell rope that has coloured wool woven into it to provide a grip for the bell-ringer's hands.
example: The teaching of the ringing of the backstroke ends when you can confidently let the learner ring it without intervention and you feel that he can set the bell at will and he can recover if the sally is not pulled with the correct strength.

definitions: Cut a screw thread in or on (a hole, screw, bolt, etc.)
Pass a thread through the eye of (a needle) or through the needle and guides of (a sewing machine)
example: The second thread quickly joins the first so that the major portion of the screw remains single threaded.

definitions: Legally take (another's child) and bring it up as one's own.
Take on or assume (an attitude or position)
example: Some time before I got to know her, Mona adopted a baby girl named Ayesha.

definitions: A violent quarrel between family members, especially a couple.
A person who is paid to help with cleaning and other menial tasks in a person's home.
example: The wage for 20 days was much higher than the normal salary for domestics.

definitions: A yellowish-brown colour.
A brown or darkened shade of skin developed after exposure to the sun.
example: Reddish brown to yellowish tan, they partly cover themselves with sand or mud to disappear from the view of their unwary prey.

definitions: A supporter or companion.
A group of people with a shared characteristic.
example: young Jack arrived with three of his cohorts

definitions: A person's sense of identity with someone or something.
The action or process of identifying someone or something or the fact of being identified.
example: He was the preeminent actor of the jeans and torn t-shirt generation who sought a total identification with the characters they played.
